title: Cowboys, Seahawks face toughest 2026 NFL schedules
body:
The National Football League has released its highly anticipated 2026 schedule, and several teams are facing a daunting task ahead. A thorough examination of the upcoming season's matchups reveals that five teams, including the Dallas Cowboys and Seattle Seahawks, have been handed particularly tough slates. These squads will have to navigate a minefield of challenging opponents, many of whom enjoyed successful campaigns in the previous season. With the NFL's competitive landscape constantly shifting, it remains to be seen how these teams will fare against their formidable foes.

One of the primary factors contributing to the perceived difficulty of a team's schedule is the number of games against playoff contenders from the previous year. In this regard, the Cowboys and Seahawks are among the most affected, with both teams facing a plethora of opponents who made deep postseason runs in 2025. The Cowboys, in particular, will have to contend with a division that has grown increasingly competitive, with their NFC East rivals having strengthened their rosters during the offseason. Meanwhile, the Seahawks will have to navigate a tough NFC West, where they will face off against perennial powerhouses like the San Francisco 49ers and Los Angeles Rams.

Another key aspect to consider when evaluating the toughness of a team's schedule is the number of games played on the road, especially against tough opponents. Road games are notoriously difficult to win in the NFL, and teams that are forced to travel frequently often struggle to maintain a high level of performance. In this regard, the Seahawks may have a slight disadvantage, as they will be playing several of their toughest games away from home. On the other hand, the Cowboys will have the benefit of playing many of their most challenging matchups at their home stadium, AT&T Stadium, where they have historically enjoyed a significant advantage.

The other three teams that round out the list of squads facing the toughest schedules are the Green Bay Packers, the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, and the Kansas City Chiefs. Each of these teams has its own unique set of challenges to overcome, from the Packers' difficult divisional schedule to the Buccaneers' daunting slate of non-conference opponents. The Chiefs, meanwhile, will have to contend with the aftermath of a potentially grueling postseason run, which could leave them vulnerable to a slow start in 2026. As the season approaches, it will be fascinating to see how these teams adapt to their difficult schedules and whether they can emerge as contenders despite the obstacles in their path.

As the NFL season draws near, fans and pundits alike will be closely watching these five teams to see how they respond to their challenging schedules. While it is impossible to predict with certainty how any team will perform, one thing is clear: the teams with the toughest schedules will have to be at the top of their game if they hope to succeed. With the NFL's competitive balance being what it is, even the most daunting schedule can be overcome with determination, hard work, and a bit of luck. As the 2026 season kicks off, all eyes will be on these five teams as they embark on their journey to overcome adversity and achieve greatness.

In conclusion, the 2026 NFL schedule has thrown down the gauntlet to several teams, challenging them to rise to the occasion and prove their mettle against the league's best opponents. As the season unfolds, it will be exciting to see how the Cowboys, Seahawks, Packers, Buccaneers, and Chiefs respond to their difficult schedules and whether they can emerge as legitimate contenders for the championship. With the NFL's reputation for unpredictability, one thing is certain: the 2026 season promises to be an thrilling ride, full of twists and turns that will keep fans on the edge of their seats until the very end.
